item.title: Fijian Drua ready to turn the tables on defending champions - Captain
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side will be looking to avenge their loss to the NSW Waratahs in round 1 when they meet this Saturday in the Super W Grand Final in Ba. Speaking from their home-base in Nadi, Captain Kolora Lomaini says t he Waratahs are a quality side and they defeated them during the pool stages however, the final presents a completely different ball game.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side will be looking to avenge their loss to the NSW Waratahs in round 1 when they meet this Saturday in the Super W Grand Final in Ba.

Speaking from their home-base in Nadi, Captain Kolora Lomaini says t he Waratahs are a quality side and they defeated them during the pool stages however, the final presents a completely different ball game.

She says the players are working hard to change that result.

Lomani says the Waratahs are the defending champions and they are well aware of the challenge they face this weekend.

She says the team has prepared thoroughly and is determined to deliver a strong performance in front of their home fans.

Lomani says they will maintain their brand of rugby and the game plan in place to counter the Waratahs, and she is urging fans to come out in numbers and cheer the team on.

She says the fans are the heartbeat of the team.

Lomani says her parents will be travelling from Australia to watch her for the final.

The Fijian Drua will face the Waratahs at 1pm at the 4R Stadium at Govind Park in Ba on Saturday.

#FIJIAN_DRUA_WOMEN | Fijian Drua Captain Kolora Lomani pleads for home support ahead of final in Ba when the Drua faces the Waratahs at 1pm at the 4R Stadium. 🎥 Video: Waisake Koroi, fijivillage News Have a news tip or captured something happening in your community? Send your photos and videos to us via Facebook Messenger, Instagram or email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Vunimono High U-17 edge DAV College 1-0 to maintain winning start
item.description: Vunimono High School's Under-17 side defeated DAV College 1-0 in the second match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools final play-Offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori. Both teams battled hard in an evenly contested first half.
item.content:encoded:
Vunimono High School's Under-17 side defeated DAV College 1-0 in the second match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools final play-Offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori.

Both teams battled hard in an evenly contested first half.

Vunimono has secured all three points and continue their winning start to the competition.

The competition continues at Ratu Cakobau Park, where teams will battle for the remaining qualification spots.

item.title: Vunimono High U15 thrash MGM 4-1 in Southern Secondary Schools final playoff
item.description: Vunimono High School's Under-15 side defeated Mahatama Gandhi Memorial High School 4-1 in the opening match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools Football Association final play-offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori this morning. Both teams created several scoring opportunities in an entertaining first half, with the scores locked at 1-1 at the break.
item.content:encoded:
Vunimono High School's Under-15 side defeated Mahatama Gandhi Memorial High School 4-1 in the opening match of the Fiji FA Southern Secondary Schools Football Association final play-offs at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori this morning.

Both teams created several scoring opportunities in an entertaining first half, with the scores locked at 1-1 at the break.

Vunimono raised their intensity after half-time, scoring three unanswered goals to seal an emphatic victory and make a strong start to their campaign.

The Southern Secondary Schools final play-offs bring together the top school teams from the Southern Zone, with the best-performing sides earning qualification for the Fiji FA Secondary Schools National Championship.

The competition continues at Ratu Cakobau Park, where teams will battle for the remaining qualification spots.

item.title: Fijian Drua Women will play the same brand of rugby in the final - Coach
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team will remain committed to its trademark style of rugby when it takes on the New South Wales Waratahs in this Saturday's Super W Grand Final. Speaking to the media from their home-base in Nadi, Head coach Mike Legge says the team's approach will remain the same as the one that secured them victory over the Queensland Reds in Lautoka, with the players full of con
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team will remain committed to its trademark style of rugby when it takes on the New South Wales Waratahs in this Saturday's Super W Grand Final.

Speaking to the media from their home-base in Nadi, Head coach Mike Legge says the team's approach will remain the same as the one that secured them victory over the Queensland Reds in Lautoka, with the players full of confidence and ready to execute their game plan.

As the team continue their preparation for the title decider, Legge says the overall objective remains unchanged and that is to win the championship on home soil.

Legge added that the squad has worked extremely hard to earn its place in the grand final and is excited about the opportunity to play in front of its home supporters.

The Fijian Drua Women will face the Waratahs this Saturday at 1pm at 4R Stadium at Govind Park in Ba.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys to face New Zealand in OFC U-16 Championship final
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys will take on New Zealand in the final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship this Saturday after both teams secured impressive semi-final victories and qualified for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup. Fiji booked their place in the final after defeating the Solomon Islands 4-2 in a dramatic penalty shootout following a goalless semi-final while New Zealand secured their place in the fi
item.content:encoded:
The Junior Bula Boys will take on New Zealand in the final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship this Saturday after both teams secured impressive semi-final victories and qualified for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup.

Fiji booked their place in the final after defeating the Solomon Islands 4-2 in a dramatic penalty shootout following a goalless semi-final while New Zealand secured their place in the final with a convincing 3-0 win over hosts Papua New Guinea in the second semi-final.

Captain Jone Loga who was also named player of the match expresses his gratitude to God for the achievement and described qualifying for the FIFA U-17 World Cup as a dream come true.

Cruz Pietersen of New Zealand was named Player of the Match.

The OFC U-16 Men's Championship final between Fiji and New Zealand will be played at 9.30pm Saturday at PNG Football Stadium in Port Moresby.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys reach OFC U-16 final, seal U-17 World Cup berth
item.description: The Junior Bula Boys have booked their place in the final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship and secured qualification for the 202

item.title: Young aiming to break personal best at first Commonwealth Games
item.description: The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games. Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.
item.content:encoded:
The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games.

Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.

She says the team constantly encourages one another, making the preparation more enjoyable.

Although this will be her first Commonwealth Games, Young has previously represented Fiji at the Commonwealth Youth Games.

She says competing against world-class athletes will be a big challenge, but believes racing against the best will bring out the best in her.

Young will compete in the women's 100 metres and 200 metres.

She also paid tribute to her training partner, Erol Qaqa, saying his positive attitude, work ethic and energy during training continue to inspire and motivate her, especially on the toughest days.

Young says she is taking her preparation one training session at a time while remaining focused on achieving a new personal best in Glasgow.

The Glasgow Commonwealth Games will kick off this Friday and end on the 2nd of August.

The women's 100 metres event is scheduled for Monday, 27th July , from 10am to 1pm, while the women's 200 metres will be held on Wednesday, 29th July , also from 10am to 1pm.

item.title: Govt and Chinese Embassy support Fiji U18 girls' historic World Championship campaign
item.description: The team presented their iTautau today, seeking the Government's blessing as they depart tonight for Romania. Speaking during the traditional ceremony at the Ministry of Youth and Sports, Minister for Housing Maciu Nalumisa acknowledged the team and officials for presenting the iTautau ceremony, saying it is an important part of Fijian culture and tradition.
item.content:encoded:
The team presented their iTautau today, seeking the Government's blessing as they depart tonight for Romania.

Speaking during the traditional ceremony at the Ministry of Youth and Sports, Minister for Housing Maciu Nalumisa acknowledged the team and officials for presenting the iTautau ceremony, saying it is an important part of Fijian culture and tradition.

Nalumisa says the ceremony carries the Government's blessing and that blessings will follow when traditions are observed in the right way.

He says Fiji's identity and cultural values define who Fijians are, and the players must uphold those values when they travel overseas.

The Minister reminded the Under-18 girls that they will not only be representing Fiji but also their individual families, urging them to make the country proud.

Nalumisa also acknowledged the sacrifices made by the players and their families to prepare for the tournament and encouraged the team to give their best, regardless of the result.

Quoting former United States President John F. Kennedy, Nalumisa told the players, "Ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country," encouraging them to proudly serve Fiji.

Meanwhile, Head of Delegation Lusiana Rokoura says the team is excited and eager to compete after months of preparation.

Rokoura says the girls qualified for the World Championship after winning the Oceania Youth Championships in the Cook Islands last October and are looking forward to testing themselves against some of the world's best teams.

She says Fiji has been drawn alongside world number four France, Egypt and Croatia, and while the challenge will be tough, the team's goal is to become the best versions of themselves and gain valuable international experience.

Rokoura says most of the players are still in high school, making the opportunity even more significant for their development.

She says the team will spend a few days in Hong Kong to acclimatise before travelling to Romania, adding that while the players are nervous, they are equally determined to make history.

Rokoura thanked the Government for providing funding through the Office of the Prime Minister Grant, saying it ensured the 12-member team could proudly represent Fiji at the World Championship.

Rokoura says without the Government's support, the historic campaign would not have been possible.

She also acknowledged the Embassy of the People's Republic of China in Fiji for its timely financial assistance, saying it enabled the team's four-day stopover in Hong Kong to complete visa processing before travelling to Romania.

Rokoura says the Embassy's support came at a critical time and gave the team hope to keep their dream of competing on the world stage alive.

Looking beyond the tournament, Rokoura says Fiji Handball plans to strengthen the sport through more awareness programmes across the country, increased school competitions and a stronger pathway from grassroots to the national team.

She says Fiji's participation at the World Championship is a historic milestone not only for the country but for the Pacific region, and the team is honoured to represent both Fiji and the region on the world stage.

After meeting Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka at the Office of the Prime Minister today, Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs Lenora Qereqeretabua stopped by to wish her cousin, Rokoura, and the Fiji Under-18 girls' team all the best ahead of their historic campaign at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship in Romania.

Qereqeretabua also gave a donation to support the team's preparations and participation in the tournament.

The Fiji Under-18 girls will begin their historic IHF Women's Youth World Championship campaign against world number four France on 29th July.

They will then face hosts Croatia on 30th July before taking on Egypt on the 1st of August in their final pool match.

Fiji is making its first appearance at the tournament, becoming the first Pacific nation to compete at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship.

item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women Head Coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged 23-member squad for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s Grand F

item.title: Family struggles fuel Rainibogi's Commonwealth Games drive
item.description: The financial challenges faced by his family are giving Fiji weightlifter Taniela Rainibogi extra motivation as he prepares for hi
